description: Fix MARSHAL bug: In online mode, moves are sent immediately to server with no undo capability. The fix: buffer moves locally during marshal mandate, provide undo (revert to state at turn start), and only commit to server when Terminar (end turn) is clicked. Same for fortress building - buffer locally, include in undo scope.
steps: 1. In src/store/gameStore.ts GAME_STATE handler (~line 2797): When receiving a state where marshalMandateActive=true and the current marshal resolution player is the localPlayer, save undoMandateState as a deep copy of the received state. This gives us the 'start of marshal turn' snapshot for undo. Add this logic: check if state.marshalMandateActive && marshalResolutionOrder[marshalResolutionIndex] === localPlayerId, then set undoMandateState to JSON.parse(JSON.stringify(state)).

2. In src/store/gameStore.ts doMoveForces (~line 2705): For online mode during marshalMandateActive, do NOT send MOVE_FIGURE to server. Instead, apply moveForces() locally (same as hotseat path). Remove the early return for online mode when marshalMandateActive is true. The existing hotseat logic after the early-return already does `const ns = moveForces(...)` and sets state - this will now also apply to online marshal.

3. In src/store/gameStore.ts doBuildFortress (~line 1295): For online mode during marshalMandateActive, do NOT send BUILD_FORTRESS to server immediately. Instead, apply buildFortress() locally (same as hotseat). Remove the early return for online mode when marshalMandateActive is true.

4. In src/store/gameStore.ts doSkipMarshalTurn (~line 1268): For online mode, instead of just sending SKIP_MARSHAL_TURN, compute the diff between current local gameState and undoMandateState. Extract: (a) all figure position changes (moves made), (b) any fortresses built. Send these as payload with SKIP_MARSHAL_TURN: { type: 'SKIP_MARSHAL_TURN', playerId, payload: { moves: [{from, to, figureIds},...], fortresses: [{provinceId},...] } }. The server will replay these moves.

5. In src/server/index.ts SKIP_MARSHAL_TURN handler (~line 1013): Modify to accept an optional payload with moves and fortresses arrays. If payload.moves exists, replay each move using moveForces(). If payload.fortresses exists, replay each using buildFortress(). Then call skipMarshalTurn() to advance. If no payload (backward compatibility), just call skipMarshalTurn() as before.

6. The undo button in ActionPanel already works - it checks undoMandateState and calls doUndoMandate which restores the saved state. Since we now set undoMandateState in the GAME_STATE handler for online marshal, the undo button will appear and work correctly.

7. In the GAME_STATE handler uiResets section: When marshalMandateActive becomes false (mandate ended), clear undoMandateState to null. This is already handled by the existing uiResets that clear moveMode etc.

8. In src/server/index.ts MOVE_FIGURE handler: Add a guard - if marshalMandateActive is true, ignore individual MOVE_FIGURE messages (since they should now come batched via SKIP_MARSHAL_TURN). Or just leave it as-is for backward compatibility - the client simply won't send them during marshal anymore.
acceptance_criteria: In online marshal mode, moving figures updates locally without sending to server
The undo button appears during online marshal and reverts all moves to turn start
Building a fortress during online marshal is applied locally and included in undo scope
Clicking Terminar sends all accumulated moves and fortress builds to server in one batch
Server correctly replays the moves and fortress builds, then advances marshal resolution
Other players see the moves only after Terminar is clicked (not during)
